shlokas.name: Ekadashi Vrat Sankalpa
shlokas.purpose: Declaration of intent before beginning the Ekadashi fast
shlokas.when_to_chant: On the morning of Ekadashi, before sunrise or at sunrise, after bathing
shlokas.english_meaning: For the destruction of all my sins, for long life, strength, good health, and prosperity, for the attainment of the four goals of life (Dharma, Artha, Kama, Moksha), and for the grace of Lord Vishnu — I now undertake the Ekadashi fast.
shlokas.notes: This sankalpa is spoken while holding water in your cupped hands. After completing the sankalpa, release the water into a vessel or into the earth.

shlokas.name: Vishnu Ashtottara Sata Namashtaka (8 names for Ekadashi)
shlokas.purpose: Short recitation of 8 names of Vishnu — ideal for those who cannot recite the full Sahasranama
shlokas.when_to_chant: During Ekadashi puja; after bathing; as many times as possible throughout the day
shlokas.sanskrit:
ओं विष्णवे नमः । ओं जनार्दनाय नमः । ओं वासुदेवाय नमः ।
ओं नारायणाय नमः । ओं श्रीधराय नमः । ओं हृषीकेशाय नमः ।
ओं पद्मनाभाय नमः । ओं दामोदराय नमः ॥
shlokas.iast:
oṃ viṣṇave namaḥ | oṃ janārdanāya namaḥ | oṃ vāsudevāya namaḥ |
oṃ nārāyaṇāya namaḥ | oṃ śrīdharāya namaḥ | oṃ hṛṣīkeśāya namaḥ |
oṃ padmanābhāya namaḥ | oṃ dāmodarāya namaḥ ||
shlokas.english_meaning: Salutations to Vishnu | Salutations to Janardana (who liberates beings) | Salutations to Vasudeva | Salutations to Narayana | Salutations to Sridhara (bearer of Lakshmi) | Salutations to Hrishikesha (lord of the senses) | Salutations to Padmanabha (lotus-navel) | Salutations to Damodara (the one with a rope around His waist — Krishna).

word_meanings.janārdana: one who grants liberation to devotees
word_meanings.nārāyaṇa: abode of all beings; refuge of all
word_meanings.śrīdhara: bearer of Lakshmi on his chest
word_meanings.padmanābha: one with a lotus in his navel from which Brahma was born
word_meanings.dāmodara: one whose belly was tied with a rope (by Yashoda as Krishna)

shlokas.name: Parana Shloka (Fast-Breaking Prayer)
shlokas.purpose: Chanted the next morning (Dwadashi) when breaking the Ekadashi fast
shlokas.when_to_chant: On Dwadashi morning, before eating, during the auspicious Parana window (shown in app)
shlokas.sanskrit:
अज्ञानात् कृतपापानां ज्ञानाद् वा सुरपुङ्गव ।
पारणं कुरु देवेश एकादश्याः फलं मम ॥
shlokas.iast:
ajñānāt kṛtapāpānāṃ jñānād vā surapuṅgava |
pāraṇaṃ kuru deveśa ekādaśyāḥ phalaṃ mama ||
shlokas.english_meaning: O Best among the gods, O Lord of the gods — for the sins I have committed knowingly or unknowingly, please bestow upon me the complete fruit of the Ekadashi fast as I now break it.
shlokas.notes: After chanting, eat Tulsi leaves dissolved in water, then eat food. Ideally break fast with sesame, banana, or other sattvic food. Avoid rice on Dwadashi.

shlokas.name: Ekadashi Mahima — Greatness of Ekadashi
shlokas.purpose: Understanding the glory and significance of Ekadashi — for reading or listening
shlokas.when_to_chant: During the day of Ekadashi; while keeping vigil at night (jaagran)
shlokas.sanskrit:
एकादश्यां न भुञ्जीत पक्षयोरुभयोरपि ।
प्राप्नोति ब्रह्महत्यायास्तत्पापं नात्र संशयः ॥
shlokas.iast:
ekādaśyāṃ na bhuñjīta pakṣayorobhayorapi |
prāpnoti brahmahatyāyāstatpāpaṃ nātra saṃśayaḥ ||
shlokas.english_meaning: One who eats on Ekadashi of either paksha (fortnight) incurs the sin equivalent to killing a Brahmin. There is no doubt about this.
shlokas.notes: This verse from the Puranas describes the gravity of breaking the Ekadashi fast — meant to motivate observance, not to cause fear.
